header.heading: What's next for you
header.subhead_complete: You finished your training. Here are ways your district and these programs can invest in what comes next — for your career and your life.
header.subhead_preview: When you finish your training, this is here for you — ways your district and these programs can invest in what comes next, for your career and your life.
header.partner_badge: Offered by external partners · not provided by CN Learning Studio

tiers.title: Build confidence at home
tiers.framing: The one benefit everyone deserves — practical help to feel steady about money, on your terms.

resources.name: SmartDollar by Ramsey Solutions
resources.description: Short lessons and private coaching to help you build an emergency fund, reduce debt, and feel confident about money — available in Spanish.

tiers.title: Grow in school nutrition
tiers.framing: Hours you need anyway — framed as growth, from the people who train school nutrition pros across the country.

resources.name: USDA Professional Standards Training Tracker
resources.description: Find free trainings that count toward your required USDA professional development hours.

resources.name: Institute of Child Nutrition (ICN) Free Training
resources.description: Free courses from the national training center — food safety, nutrition basics, and management skills.

resources.name: SNA Certificate Program
resources.description: Earn a nationally recognized school nutrition credential — starts at $20 for SNA members.

tiers.title: Build what comes next
tiers.framing: A real credential, at your own pace — from names that change how the world sees your work.

resources.name: Google Career Certificates
resources.description: No degree needed. Earn a Google credential in data, IT, or project management — at your own pace.

resources.name: IBM & Meta Professional Certificates
resources.description: Professional certificates from IBM, Meta, and more — build skills for roles beyond the cafeteria.

resources.name: Coursera Financial Aid
resources.description: Can't afford the fee? Coursera offers financial aid — most applications are approved.
